article_meta.title: Elections Unsealed Court Docs Show The FBI Probing Possible Election Crimes In Fulton County By: M.D

reasoning: The summary avoids the article’s loaded rhetoric and uses neutral, attributed language (e.g., quoting “extremely sloppy,” noting some allegations disproven). However, it under-represents countervailing perspectives (e.g., Washington Post critique, election officials’ stance) and lists only the outlet, not distinct perspectives, in sources_represented.

custom_reasoning.spin: Verbs and tone are mostly clinical; no adoption of the article’s pejorative phrasing.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: One claim (“ballots counted multiple times”) is presented without explicit attribution in the summary line, though cited in key facts.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative statements are quoted or attributed; none asserted in model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No alarming or emotive language beyond quoted terms.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions beyond the affidavit’s stated purpose.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes investigative allegations; underplays/omits critical counter-perspectives present in the article.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Does not include Washington Post’s critique, David Becker’s view, or election officials’ position that issues didn’t affect outcomes.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Only uses subjective phrasing in quotes (“extremely sloppy”).
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Focus is on discrepancies and failures; minimal balancing context.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No systematic preference for elites or grassroots.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Language is notably more neutral than the source; avoids loaded descriptors.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Sources_represented lists only the publication, omitting multiple distinct perspectives included in the article.
